channel.title: humux blog
channel.description: Deep dives into agent architecture, memory systems, and self-hosted infrastructure.

item.title: Release Notes: v0.25 – v0.28
item.description: Four releases in one week: GitHub webhook integration, per-agent LLM overrides, token usage dashboard, and chain-of-thought feedback.

item.title: How I Replaced My Cloud AI Assistant with a Self-Hosted Agent
item.description: A personal account of migrating from cloud AI assistants to humux — what worked, what broke, and what I'd do differently.

item.title: Subagent Patterns: Scoped Delegation for Complex Tasks
item.description: How to spawn scoped sub-tasks with inherit-never-widen security, budget controls, and sync or background execution.

item.title: Group Chat Engineering: Multiple Agents, Zero Loops
item.description: The addressing protocol, reply decision gate, rate cap, and speaker tags that make multi-agent Telegram groups work.

item.title: Why Multiple Agents Beat One Generalist
item.description: Specialized agents with different skills, tools, and personalities outperform one monolithic agent trying to do everything.

item.title: Four Workflows, One Agent: Use Case Walkthroughs
item.description: Real conversation flows for four personas — from code review to email triage to multi-agent group chats.

item.title: Voice Pipelines for Agents: On-Device STT and TTS
item.description: Building offline-capable voice interaction with faster-whisper for speech-to-text and Kokoro 82M for text-to-speech.

item.title: Secrets Management for AI Agents: Why .env Files Aren't Enough
item.description: How a two-tier encrypted vault keeps credentials out of the model context and why .env files are a security risk for autonomous agents.

item.title: Permission Engineering for Autonomous Agents
item.description: Glob-pattern rules, approval flows via Telegram, and vault-sealed secrets that never enter the model context.

item.title: Memory Architecture for Long-Running Agents
item.description: Four tiers of memory: lexical retrieval, semantic search, importance-based forgetting, and hygiene via deduplication.

item.title: Skills Over Code: Teaching Agents via Markdown
item.description: Why describing CLI tools in markdown files beats writing Python adapter classes for every new agent capability.

item.title: Running a Full Agent Stack in a Single Container
item.description: Architecture decisions that make it possible to run LLM orchestration, voice, email, calendar, and admin UI in one Docker container.

item.title: Designing Multi-Channel Agent Architectures
item.description: How to build agents that operate across Telegram, email, calendar, and CLI as a unified system with a common message abstraction.
